excerpt: The sea denotes abundance and the distinctive character that pervades all things. The world presents countless occasions for transgression; the soul generates desires in waves, and without intellect, the nafs leads toward perdition.
When you recite verses of mercy, your heart yearns for the blessings of Allah; when you recite verses of punishment, your body trembles in awe of Him.
The fulfillment of faith is achieved only through continuous struggle and perseverance; life's endeavor is wasted if it does not end in the wilaya of Ali and his descendants.
source_location_hint: teachings on completion of religious acts and wilaya

excerpt: Humankind has been granted the rank of noblest of all creatures through the intellect, which grants discernment and choice. Nobility derives not from mere possession but from rightful use of wisdom in beliefs, intentions, knowledge, and actions.
source_location_hint: doctrine of intellect and human nobility

excerpt: The beginning of wrath is derangement, and its end is regret; he who does not regret has remained in madness. The best among you is he who grows angry yet quickly becomes appeased.
The most difficult act is forgiveness in the midst of wrath; to forgive whilst possessing the power of retribution. Forgiveness is the purification of victory.

Backbiting consumes righteous deeds and devours virtues. God compares backbiting to consuming one's dead brother's flesh to emphasize its repugnance. The hearer of backbiting becomes an equal partner in sin.
source_location_hint: ethical teachings on major and minor sins

excerpt: The heart devoted to worldly pursuits receives anger, humiliation, and calamity. The heart contemplating the Hereafter shall receive Paradise. If pursuit of the world brings misery, then attainment of religion brings mercy, honor, and blessing.
source_location_hint: exposition on three types of hearts and their destinies

excerpt: Jihad in God's path encompasses jihad of the self, jihad through wealth, and struggle to attain knowledge of God's Oneness. To know the limits and to exercise rights accordingly is what true striving in God signifies.

The verses lament the loss of beloved ones to death—first the Prophet and then his daughter—as proof that no human bond, however strong, can withstand the separation that death brings; all companionship must dissolve.
source_location_hint: elegiac verses on mortality and the Prince of the Faithful
